rationale: Add a procedural guardrail to prefer temp files over long inline CLI arguments, reducing Windows arg-length failures without touching code.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedural_guardrail.v1",
  "title": "Windows CLI argument length guardrail",
  "rules": [
    "Prefer writing large payloads (JSON, prompts, configs) to temp files and pass file paths instead of inline CLI arguments.",
    "Avoid embedding long JSON or base64 blobs in command lines; use @file or stdin redirection when supported.",
    "If a tool requires inline args, truncate or summarize and reference a file for full content.",
    "When constructing commands, aim to keep total argument length well below typical Windows limits."
  ],
  "applies_to": ["exec", "process", "sessions_spawn"],
  "priority": "high"
}

risks: New guardrail may be ignored if not consulted by current workflows.
expected_benefit: Reduces risk of Windows command-line length errors and improves execution reliability for large payloads.
